CREATE TABLE test_strings (haystack String, pattern String, replacement String);
SELECT replaceOne(haystack, pattern, replacement) FROM test_strings;
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES ('foo bar foo', 'foo', 'baz');
SELECT replaceOne(haystack, 'bar', 'qux') AS replaced_string FROM test_strings WHERE haystack LIKE '%bar%';
CREATE TABLE another_table (id UInt32, info String);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, 'foo bar'), (2, 'hello world'), (3, 'bar foo');
SELECT a.id, replaceOne(a.info, 'foo', 'baz') AS updated_info FROM another_table AS a JOIN test_strings AS b ON a.info LIKE concat('%', b.pattern, '%');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES ('this is a test', 'test', 'example');
SELECT replaceOne(haystack, 'a', 'A') FROM test_strings WHERE haystack = 'this is a test';
SELECT replaceOne(replaceOne(haystack, 'foo', 'baz'), 'bar', 'qux') FROM test_strings WHERE haystack = 'foo bar';
SELECT replaceOne(haystack, 'test', 'TEST'), replaceOne(haystack, 'nonexistent', 'value') FROM test_strings WHERE haystack LIKE '%test%';
